name: multi_agent_ollama_peer_memory
description: Two-agent deployment: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Agent A, main) with Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 (Agent B, peer). Inter-agent messages are logged (= part of T̃_t). Agent B's private memory/scratchpad is unlogged and feeds into Agent A's operative state S_t. A single unlogged edge peer_scratch → S_t gives the min-cut: ε_state^UB = d_B · log₂(Q) = 4096 · 8 = 32768 bits.
notes: Inter-agent message channel (message_b_to_a) is logged — visible to auditor.
Agent B's private scratchpad (agent_b_scratch → S_t) is the single unlogged channel.
Min-cut: single edge agent_b_scratch → S_t. Capacity = 4096 · 8 = 32768 bits.
This single-hidden-channel design avoids parallel-cut semantics ambiguity: there is exactly one unlogged path from source to sink.
Per-edge budget uses Q=256 (8-bit audit discretization).
